entry.title: Deep Entity Handling in SAP UI5 Using OData Service
entry.content:
• Introduction:

A Deep Entity allows you to send and receive hierarchical data (parent + child) in a single request, making your application more efficient, cleaner, and closer to real business scenarios.

In this blog, we will build a complete end-to-end example of a  Deep Entity, covering:

  • Backend implementation using SEGW (ABAP OData)
  • Frontend implementation using SAP UI5
  • Read (Expand) and Create (Deep Insert) operations

Architecture Overview:

SAP UI5 App 

   ↓ 

OData Service (Gateway) 

   ↓ 

Backend Logic (ABAP) 

   ↓ 

Database Tables 

Backend Implementation (ABAP - OData Service):

Step 1: Define Entities in SEGW:

Create: 

  • Header Entity → Sales Order (VBAK) 

  • Item Entity → Sales Order Item (VBAP) 

Step 2: Create Association :

  • Association: Header → Item (1: N)  

  • Navigation Property: znavigation.

Step 3: Create Deep Structure: 

  • Go To: MPC EXT → Source Code-Based. 
  • Create Deep Structure.

 

class ZCL_ZK_DEEP_ENTITY_MPC_EXT definition 
   public 
   inheriting from ZCL_ZK_DEEP_ENTITY_MPC 
   create public . 
public section. 
  types: 
     BEGIN OF ty_deep_str, 
               vbeln              TYPE  vbeln_va, 
               erdat              TYPE  erdat, 
               ernam              TYPE  ernam, 
               auart              TYPE  auart, 
               kunnr              TYPE  kunnr, 
               znavigation TYPE TABLE OF zcl_zk_deep_entity_mpc_ext=>ts_vbap WITH DEFAULT KEY, 
             END OF ty_deep_str . 
protected section. 
   PRIVATE SECTION. 
ENDCLASS. 

CLASS ZCL_ZK_DEEP_ENTITY_MPC_EXT IMPLEMENTATION. 
ENDCLASS.

 

 Step 3: Redefine GET_EXPANDED_ENTITY, GET_EXPANDED_ENTITYSET, CREATE_DEEP_ENTITY. 

Go to: 

DPC_EXT Class → Method: 

Method: /IWBEP/IF_MGW_APPL_SRV_RUNTIME~GET_EXPANDED_ENTITY. 

 

METHOD /iwbep/if_mgw_appl_srv_runtime~get_expanded_entity. 
 
  DATA: ls_deep_str TYPE zcl_zk_deep_entity_mpc_ext=>ty_deep_str, 
         ls_vbak     TYPE zstr_vbak, 
         lt_vbap     TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zstr_vbap. 
 
  DATA lv_vbeln TYPE vbeln_va. 
 
  "-------------------------------------------------- 
   " Read key 
   "-------------------------------------------------- 
   lv_vbeln = VALUE #( it_key_tab[ name = 'Vbeln' ]-value OPTIONAL ). 
   lv_vbeln = |{ lv_vbeln ALPHA = IN }|. 
 
  "-------------------------------------------------- 
   " Read Header (VBAK) 
   "-------------------------------------------------- 
   SELECT SINGLE 
          vbeln, 
          erdat, 
          ernam, 
          auart, 
          kunnr 
     FROM zdt_vbak 
     INTO _vbak 
     WHERE vbeln = _vbeln. 
 
  IF sy-subrc <> 0. 
     RETURN. 
   ENDIF. 
 
  "-------------------------------------------------- 
   " Read Items (VBAP) 
   "-------------------------------------------------- 
   SELECT 
          vbeln, 
          posnr, 
          matnr, 
          kwmeng, 
          vrkme 
     FROM zdt_vbap 
     INTO TABLE _vbap 
     WHERE vbeln = _vbeln. 
 
  "-------------------------------------------------- 
   " Explicit Header Mapping (IMPORTANT) 
   "-------------------------------------------------- 
   CLEAR ls_deep_str. 
 
  ls_deep_str-vbeln = ls_vbak-vbeln. 
   ls_deep_str-erdat = ls_vbak-erdat. 
   ls_deep_str-ernam = ls_vbak-ernam. 
   ls_deep_str-auart = ls_vbak-auart. 
   ls_deep_str-kunnr = ls_vbak-kunnr. 
 
  "-------------------------------------------------- 
   " Items Mapping 
   "-------------------------------------------------- 
   IF lt_vbap IS NOT INITIAL. 
     ls_deep_str-znavigation = CORRESPONDING #( lt_vbap ). 
   ENDIF. 
 
  "-------------------------------------------------- 
   " Return Deep Entity 
   "-------------------------------------------------- 
   copy_data_to_ref( 
     EXPORTING 
       is_data = ls_deep_str 
     CHANGING 
       cr_data = er_entity 
   ). 
 
  APPEND 'znavigation' TO et_expanded_clauses. 
 
ENDMETHOD.

 

Method: /IWBEP/IF_MGW_APPL_SRV_RUNTIME~GET_EXPANDED_ENTITYSET. 

 

  method /IWBEP/IF_MGW_APPL_SRV_RUNTIME~GET_EXPANDED_ENTITYSET. 
 
      DATA : lt_vbak TYPE ZCL_ZK_DEEP_ENTITY_MPC_EXT=>tt_vbak, 
              lt_vbap TYPE ZCL_ZK_DEEP_ENTITY_MPC_EXT=>tt_vbap, 
              ls_deep type ZCL_ZK_DEEP_ENTITY_MPC_EXT=>ty_deep_str, 
              lt_deep TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ZCL_ZK_DEEP_ENTITY_MPC_EXT=>ty_deep_str. 
 
 
        SELECT FROM zdt_vbak FIELDS VBELN, 
                                     ERDAT, 
                                     ERNAM, 
                                     AUART, 
                                     KUNNR INTO TABLE _vbak. 
 
          LOOP AT lt_vbak ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_vbak>). 

           clear  ls_deep . 
 
            ls_deep = CORRESPONDING #( <fs_vbak> ). 
 
            SELECT FROM zdt_vbap FIELDS VBELN, 
                                         POSNR, 
                                         MATNR, 
                                         KWMENG, 
                                         VRKME 
               WHERE vbeln = @<fs_vbak>-vbeln 
               INTO TABLE _vbap. 
 
              ls_deep-znavigation =  CORRESPONDING #( lt_vbak ). 
 
             APPEND ls_deep to lt_deep. 
 
          ENDLOOP. 

          copy_data_to_ref( 
             EXPORTING 
               is_data = lt_deep 
             CHANGING 
               cr_data = er_entityset 
           ). 

          APPEND 'znavigation' to et_expanded_clauses. 

  endmethod.

 

 Method: /IWBEP/IF_MGW_APPL_SRV_RUNTIME~CREATE_DEEP_ENTITY. 

 

  METHOD /iwbep/if_mgw_appl_srv_runtime~create_deep_entity. 
     DATA : ls_deep    TYPE zcl_zk_deep_entity_mpc_ext=>ty_deep_str, 
            ls_vbak    TYPE zcl_zk_deep_entity_mpc_ext=>ts_vbak, 
            ls_vbap    TYPE zcl_zk_deep_entity_mpc_ext=>ts_vbap, 
 
           ls_vbak2   TYPE zdt_vbak, 
            ls_vbap2   TYPE zdt_vbap, 
 
           lv_vbeln   TYPE zdt_vbak-vbeln, 
            lv_posnr   TYPE zdt_vbap-posnr, 
 
           lv_vbeln_n TYPE zdt_vbak-vbeln, 
            lv_posnr_n TYPE zdt_vbap-posnr. 
 
    io_data_provider->read_entry_data( 
       IMPORTING 
         es_data = ls_deep 
     ). 
 
    SELECT MAX( vbeln ) 
           INTO _vbeln 
           FROM zdt_vbak. 
 
    IF lv_vbeln IS INITIAL. 
       lv_vbeln_n = 1. 
     ELSE. 
       lv_vbeln_n = lv_vbeln + 1. 
     ENDIF. 
 
    lv_vbeln_n = |{ lv_vbeln_n ALPHA = IN }|. 
     ls_vbak-vbeln = lv_vbeln_n. 
     ls_vbak-erdat = sy-datum. 
     ls_vbak-ernam = sy-uname. 
     ls_vbak-auart = ls_deep-auart. 
     ls_vbak-kunnr = ls_deep-kunnr. 
 
    MOVE-CORRESPONDING ls_vbak TO ls_vbak2. 
 
    INSERT zdt_vbak FROM ls_vbak2. 
 
    CLEAR lv_posnr. 
 
    LOOP AT ls_deep-znavigation INTO DATA(ls_deep_item). 
       lv_posnr = lv_posnr + 10. 
 
      ls_vbap-vbeln  = lv_vbeln_n. 
       ls_vbap-posnr  = lv_posnr. 
       ls_vbap-matnr  = ls_deep_item-matnr. 
       ls_vbap-kwmeng = ls_deep_item-kwmeng. 
       ls_vbap-vrkme  = ls_deep_item-vrkme. 
 
      MOVE-CORRESPONDING ls_vbap TO ls_vbap2. 
 
      INSERT zdt_vbap FROM ls_vbap2. 
 
      CLEAR ls_vbap2. 
     ENDLOOP. 
     ls_deep-vbeln = lv_vbeln_n. 
     ls_deep-erdat = sy-datum. 
 
    copy_data_to_ref( 
       EXPORTING 
         is_data = ls_deep 
       CHANGING 
         cr_data = er_deep_entity 
     ). 

  ENDMETHOD.

 

 UI5 Implementation:

Step 1: View – Binding Data and Preparing Navigation:

  • The view shows a list of sales orders by binding the table to the header entity (vbakSet). When the screen loads, it automatically fetches only basic data (like order number, date, customer). It does not load item data here, so the screen stays fast and simple. 
  • Each row in the table is made clickable and shows a small arrow because of the navigation type. This tells the user that clicking a row will take them to another screen. 
  • When a row is clicked, the view triggers an event and sends the selected row’s data to the controller. This includes the important key field (Vbeln), which is needed to identify the sales order. 
  • From a deep entity point of view, the view does not load full data. It only shows header data and prepares the key, so that the next screen can use it to fetch complete data (header + items) together. 

 

<mvc:View
    controllerName="project1.controller.View1"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    xmlns="sap.m"
    xmlns:f="sap.f"
    displayBlock="true">

    <f:DynamicPage
        id="dynPage"
        toggleHeaderOnTitleClick="false">

        <!-- ================= TITLE ================= -->
        <f:title>
            <f:DynamicPageTitle>

                <f:heading>
                    <HBox alignItems="Center">
                        <Avatar
                            src="sap-icon://sales-order"
                            displayShape="Square"
                            class="sapUiTinyMarginEnd"/>

                        <VBox>
                            <Title text="Sales Orders" level="H2"/>
                            <Text text="Manage and track sales orders"/>
                        </VBox>
                    </HBox>
                </f:heading>

                <f:actions>
                    <Button
                        text="Create"
                        icon="sap-icon://add"
                        type="Emphasized"
                        press="onCreate"/>

                    <!--
                    <Button
                        text="Edit"
                        icon="sap-icon://edit"
                        press="onUpdate"
                        enabled="{= ${vbakTable>/selectedItems}.length === 1 }"/>
                    -->

                    <Button
                        text="Delete"
                        icon="sap-icon://delete"
                        type="Negative"
                        press="onDelete"
                        enabled="{= ${vbakTable>/selectedItems}.length > 0 }"/>
                </f:actions>

            </f:DynamicPageTitle>
        </f:title>

        <!-- ================= HEADER ================= -->
        <f:header>
            <f:DynamicPageHeader pinnable="true">

                <Toolbar design="Transparent">
                    <Label text="Search"/>
                    <SearchField
                        width="20rem"
                        liveChange="onSearch"
                        placeholder="Sales Order / Customer"/>
                </Toolbar>

            </f:DynamicPageHeader>
        </f:header>

        <!-- ================= CONTENT ================= -->
        <f:content>
            <Table
                id="vbakTable"
                items="{ path: '/vbakSet' }"
                growing="true"
                growingScrollToLoad="true"
                mode="MultiSelect"
                itemPress="onItemPress"
                sticky="ColumnHeaders"
                noDataText="No sales orders found"
                busyIndicatorDelay="0">

                <!-- ================= COLUMNS ================= -->
                <columns>
                    <Column width="11rem">
                        <Text text="Sales Order"/>
                    </Column>

                    <Column width="9rem" demandPopin="true" minScreenWidth="Tablet">
                        <Text text="Created On"/>
                    </Column>

                    <Column width="9rem" demandPopin="true" minScreenWidth="Tablet">
                        <Text text="Created By"/>
                    </Column>

                    <Column width="7rem">
                        <Text text="Type"/>
                    </Column>

                    <Column width="11rem" demandPopin="true" minScreenWidth="Desktop">
                        <Text text="Customer"/>
                    </Column>
                </columns>

                <!-- ================= ITEMS ================= -->
                <items>
                    <ColumnListItem
                        type="Navigation">

                        <cells>
                            <ObjectIdentifier
                                title="{Vbeln}"
                                titleActive="true"/>

                            <Text text="{Erdat}"/>

                            <Text text="{Ernam}"/>

                            <ObjectStatus
                                text="{Auart}"
                                state="Information"/>

                            <Text text="{Kunnr}"/>
                        </cells>

                    </ColumnListItem>
                </items>

            </Table>
        </f:content>

    </f:DynamicPage>
</mvc:View>

 

Step 2: Controller – Handling Row Selection and Parameter-Based Routing:

  • When the user clicks on a row, the controller takes that row and gets its data. From that data, it picks the Sales Order number (Vbeln), which is the main key. 
  • After getting this key, the controller performs router-based navigation using parameter-based (dynamic) routing. It sends the user to the Detail page and passes the Vbeln as a parameter, so the next screen knows exactly which sales order to load. 
  • In terms of deep entity, the controller is not loading all the data here. It is just sending the key, and using that key, the next screen will fetch full data (header + items) together. 

 

sap.ui.define(
    [
        "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller"
    ],
    function (Controller) {
        "use strict";

        return Controller.extend("project1.controller.View1", {

            onInit: function () {

            },

            onCreate: function () {
                this.getOwnerComponent().getRouter().navTo("Create");
            },

            onItemPress: function (oEvent) {
                debugger;

                var sVbeln = oEvent
                    .getParameter("listItem")
                    .getBindingContext()
                    .getProperty("Vbeln");

                this.getOwnerComponent().getRouter().navTo("Detail", {
                    Vbeln: sVbeln
                });
            }

        });
    }
);

 

 Output:

Step 3: View – Showing Header and Item Data: 

  • The view is designed to show one sales order in detail. It displays both the header data (like order number, customer, date) and the item data (list of materials). 
  • It uses a local model (detail) where data is divided into two parts: 
  • Header → main sales order 
  • Items → list of related items 
  • The header section is bound to detail>/Header, and the table is bound to detail>/Items, so it can show parent + child data together. 

 

<mvc:View
    controllerName="project1.controller.Detail"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    xmlns="sap.m"
    xmlns:uxap="sap.uxap"
    displayBlock="true">

    <uxap:ObjectPageLayout
        id="ObjectPage"
        enableLazyLoading="true"
        showHeaderContent="true"
        showAnchorBar="true"
        upperCaseAnchorBar="false"
        isChildPage="true">

        <!-- ================= HEADER ================= -->
        <uxap:headerTitle>
            <uxap:ObjectPageHeader
                objectTitle="{detail>/Header/Vbeln}"
                objectSubtitle="Sales Order"
                objectImageURI="sap-icon://sales-order"
                objectImageShape="Square"
                isObjectIconAlwaysVisible="true">

                <!-- Header Actions (Fiori standard) -->
                <uxap:actions>
                    <Button
                        icon="sap-icon://nav-back"
                        type="Emphasized"
                        tooltip="Back"
                        press="onNavBack"/>

                    <Button
                        icon="sap-icon://edit"
                        text="Edit"
                        type="Attention"/>
                </uxap:actions>

            </uxap:ObjectPageHeader>
        </uxap:headerTitle>

        <!-- ================= HEADER CONTENT ================= -->
        <uxap:headerContent>
            <HBox
                justifyContent="Start"
                alignItems="Center"
                class="sapUiSmallMargin">

                <ObjectStatus
                    title="Document Type"
                    text="{detail>/Header/Auart}"
                    state="Information"/>

            </HBox>
        </uxap:headerContent>

        <!-- ================= SECTIONS ================= -->
        <uxap:sections>

            <!-- ================= GENERAL INFORMATION ================= -->
            <uxap:ObjectPageSection
                title="General Information"
                titleUppercase="false">

                <uxap:subSections>
                    <uxap:ObjectPageSubSection>

                        <VBox class="sapUiMediumMargin sapUiResponsiveContentPadding">

                            <HBox wrap="Wrap" class="sapUiSmallMarginBottom">

                                <VBox width="18rem" class="sapUiSmallMarginEnd">
                                    <Label text="Customer"/>
                                    <ObjectIdentifier title="{detail>/Header/Kunnr}"/>
                                </VBox>

                                <VBox width="18rem" class="sapUiSmallMarginEnd">
                                    <Label text="Created On"/>
                                    <Text text="{detail>/Header/Erdat}"/>
                                </VBox>

                                <VBox width="18rem">
                                    <Label text="Created By"/>
                                    <Text text="{detail>/Header/Ernam}"/>
                                </VBox>

                            </HBox>

                        </VBox>

                    </uxap:ObjectPageSubSection>
                </uxap:subSections>
            </uxap:ObjectPageSection>

            <!-- ================= ITEMS ================= -->
            <uxap:ObjectPageSection
                title="Items"
                titleUppercase="true">

                <uxap:subSections>
                    <uxap:ObjectPageSubSection>

                        <Table
                            items="{detail>/Items}"
                            growing="true"
                            growingScrollToLoad="true"
                            sticky="HeaderToolbar,ColumnHeaders"
                            class="sapUiResponsiveContentPadding">

                            <!-- Section Toolbar (Fiori pattern) -->
                            <headerToolbar>
                                <OverflowToolbar>
                                    <Title text="Order Items" level="H3"/>
                                    <ToolbarSpacer/>

                                    <Text text="Items: {= ${detail>/Items}.length }"/>

                                    <ToolbarSeparator/>

                                    <Button
                                        icon="sap-icon://add"
                                        text="Add Item"
                                        type="Transparent"/>
                                </OverflowToolbar>
                            </headerToolbar>

                            <columns>
                                <Column width="5rem">
                                    <Text text="Item"/>
                                </Column>

                                <Column>
                                    <Text text="Material"/>
                                </Column>

                                <Column hAlign="End" width="8rem">
                                    <Text text="Quantity"/>
                                </Column>

                                <Column width="6rem">
                                    <Text text="Unit"/>
                                </Column>
                            </columns>

                            <items>
                                <ColumnListItem>
                                    <cells>
                                        <ObjectIdentifier title="{detail>Posnr}"/>
                                        <Text text="{detail>Matnr}"/>
                                        <ObjectNumber number="{detail>Kwmeng}"/>
                                        <ObjectStatus text="{detail>Vrkme}"/>
                                    </cells>
                                </ColumnListItem>
                            </items>

                        </Table>

                    </uxap:ObjectPageSubSection>
                </uxap:subSections>
            </uxap:ObjectPageSection>

        </uxap:sections>

        <!-- ================= FOOTER (Fiori standard) ================= -->
        <uxap:footer>
            <OverflowToolbar>
                <ToolbarSpacer/>

                <Button
                    text="Edit"
                    icon="sap-icon://edit"
                    type="Emphasized"/>

                <Button
                    text="Cancel"
                    type="Transparent"
                    press="onNavBack"/>
            </OverflowToolbar>
        </uxap:footer>

    </uxap:ObjectPageLayout>
</mvc:View>

 

Step 4: Controller – Fetching Deep Entity Data:

  • The controller gets the Vbeln (Sales Order number) from routing when the screen opens. 
  • Using this key, it makes one OData call with $expand, which fetches: 
  • Header data 
  • Item data 

in a single request (deep entity). 

  • After getting the data, it splits it into: 
  • Header → stored separately 
  • Items → stored separately 

and sets it into the local model, which is then shown in the view. 

 

sap.ui.define(
    [
        "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller",
        "sap/ui/model/json/JSONModel"
    ],
    function (Controller, JSONModel) {
        "use strict";

        return Controller.extend("project1.controller.Detail", {

            onInit: function () {
                debugger;

                this.getOwnerComponent()
                    .getRouter()
                    .getRoute("Detail")
                    .attachPatternMatched(this._onObjectMatched, this);

                this.getView().setModel(
                    new JSONModel({
                        Header: {},
                        Items: []
                    }),
                    "detail"
                );
            },

            _onObjectMatched: function (oEvent) {
                debugger;

                var sVbeln = oEvent.getParameter("arguments").Vbeln;

                sVbeln = sVbeln.padStart(10, "0");

                var oModel = this.getOwnerComponent().getModel();
                var oDetailModel = this.getView().getModel("detail");

                oModel.read("/vbakSet('" + sVbeln + "')", {
                    urlParameters: {
                        "$expand": "znavigation"
                    },
                    success: function (oData) {
                        debugger;

                        oDetailModel.setProperty("/Header", oData);
                        oDetailModel.setProperty(
                            "/Items",
                            oData.znavigation.results
                        );
                    },
                    error: function () {
                        debugger;
                    }
                });
            },

            onNavBack: function () {
                this.getOwnerComponent()
                    .getRouter()
                    .navTo("RouteView1");
            }

        });
    }
);

 

 Output:

Step 5: View – Entering Header and Item Data:

  • The view is designed to create a new sales order. It allows the user to enter both header data (like document type, customer) and item data (materials, quantity, unit) in the same screen. 
  • It uses a local model (local) to store user input. The header fields are directly bound to this model, and the items table is bound to local>/Items, which holds multiple item entries. 
  • The user can dynamically add or remove items, so the view supports multiple child entries under one header. 

 

<mvc:View
    controllerName="project1.controller.Create"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    xmlns="sap.m"
    xmlns:uxap="sap.uxap"
    xmlns:form="sap.ui.layout.form">

    <uxap:ObjectPageLayout
        id="CreateSalesOrderPage"
        showHeaderContent="true">

        <!-- ================= HEADER ================= -->
        <uxap:headerTitle>
            <uxap:ObjectPageHeader
                objectTitle="Create Sales Order"
                objectSubtitle="Header and Items"
                objectImageURI="sap-icon://sales-order"
                objectImageShape="Square">

                <uxap:actions>
                    <Button
                        text="Save"
                        icon="sap-icon://save"
                        type="Emphasized"
                        press="onSave"/>

                    <Button
                        text="Cancel"
                        icon="sap-icon://cancel"
                        type="Reject"
                        press="onCancel"/>
                </uxap:actions>

            </uxap:ObjectPageHeader>
        </uxap:headerTitle>

        <!-- ================= SECTIONS ================= -->
        <uxap:sections>

            <!-- ================= HEADER DATA ================= -->
            <uxap:ObjectPageSection title="Header Data">
                <uxap:subSections>
                    <uxap:ObjectPageSubSection>

                        <form:SimpleForm
                            editable="true"
                            layout="ResponsiveGridLayout"
                            labelSpanXL="3"
                            labelSpanL="3"
                            labelSpanM="4"
                            labelSpanS="12">

                            <Label text="Sales Document Type"/>
                            <Input
                                value="{local>/Auart}"
                                id="Aurat"
                                placeholder="AUART"
                                showValueHelp="true"
                                valueHelpRequest="onAuartValueHelp"/>

                            <Label text="Customer"/>
                            <Input
                                id="Kunnr"
                                value="{local>/Kunnr}"
                                placeholder="KUNNR"/>

                            <Label text="Created On"/>
                            <DatePicker editable="false"/>

                            <Label text="Created By"/>
                            <Input editable="false"/>

                        </form:SimpleForm>

                    </uxap:ObjectPageSubSection>
                </uxap:subSections>
            </uxap:ObjectPageSection>

            <!-- ================= ITEM DATA ================= -->
            <uxap:ObjectPageSection title="Items">
                <uxap:subSections>
                    <uxap:ObjectPageSubSection>

                        <Table
                            id="itemTable"
                            mode="MultiSelect"
                            growing="true"
                            items="{
                                path: 'local>/Items'
                            }">

                            <headerToolbar>
                                <Toolbar>
                                    <Title text="Sales Order Items"/>
                                    <ToolbarSpacer/>
                                    <Button
                                        icon="sap-icon://add"
                                        text="Add Item"
                                        press="onAddItem"/>
                                    <Button
                                        icon="sap-icon://delete"
                                        text="Remove Item"
                                        press="onRemoveItem"/>
                                </Toolbar>
                            </headerToolbar>

                            <columns>
                                <Column>
                                    <Text text="Item"/>
                                </Column>
                                <Column>
                                    <Text text="Material"/>
                                </Column>
                                <Column hAlign="End">
                                    <Text text="Quantity"/>
                                </Column>
                                <Column>
                                    <Text text="Unit"/>
                                </Column>
                            </columns>

                            <items>
                                <ColumnListItem>
                                    <cells>
                                        <Input
                                            value="{local>Posnr}"
                                            editable="false"/>

                                        <Input
                                            value="{local>Matnr}"
                                            placeholder="MATNR"/>

                                        <Input
                                            type="Number"
                                            value="{local>Kwmeng}"
                                            placeholder="Quantity"/>

                                        <Input
                                            value="{local>Vrkme}"
                                            placeholder="Unit"/>
                                    </cells>
                                </ColumnListItem>
                            </items>

                        </Table>

                    </uxap:ObjectPageSubSection>
                </uxap:subSections>
            </uxap:ObjectPageSection>

        </uxap:sections>

    </uxap:ObjectPageLayout>
</mvc:View>

 

Step 6:Creating Deep Entity (Header + Items):

  • The controller manages user input and stores all item data in the local model. When the user clicks save, it collects both header and item data.  
  • It creates a single payload that contains:  
  • Header data (Auart, Kunnr, etc.)  
  • Item data (array of items)  
  • Then it makes one create a call to backend, sending everything together.  
  • After successful creation, it reads the latest sales order and navigates to the Detail page using the generated Vbeln. 

 

sap.ui.define(
    [
        "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller",
        "sap/ui/model/json/JSONModel",
        "sap/m/MessageToast"
    ],
    function (Controller, JSONModel, MessageToast) {
        "use strict";

        return Controller.extend("project1.controller.Create", {

            onInit: function () {

                // Local JSON model for dynamic items
                var oData = {
                    Items: []
                };

                var oLocalModel = new JSONModel(oData);
                this.getView().setModel(oLocalModel, "local");
            },

            /* =========================================================== */
            /* NAVIGATION */
            /* =========================================================== */

            onCancel: function () {
                this.getOwnerComponent()
                    .getRouter()
                    .navTo("RouteView1");
            },

            /* =========================================================== */
            /* VALUE HELP */
            /* =========================================================== */

            oAuartVh: null,

            onAuartValueHelp: function () {

                if (!this.oAuartVh) {
                    this.oAuartVh = sap.ui.xmlfragment(
                        "project1.Fragments.AuartVH",
                        this
                    );

                    this.getView().addDependent(this.oAuartVh);
                }

                this.oAuartVh.open();
            },

            onAuartCancel: function () {
                this.oAuartVh.close();
            },

            onAuartConfirm: function (oEvent) {

                var oItem = oEvent.getParameter("selectedItem");
                if (!oItem) {
                    return;
                }

                this.byId("Aurat").setValue(oItem.getTitle());
            },

            onExit: function () {

                if (this.oAuartVh) {
                    this.oAuartVh.destroy();
                    this.oAuartVh = null;
                }
            },

            /* =========================================================== */
            /* ADD ITEM */
            /* =========================================================== */

            onAddItem: function () {

                var oModel = this.getView().getModel("local");
                var aItems = oModel.getProperty("/Items");

                aItems.push({
                    Posnr: "",
                    Matnr: "",
                    Kwmeng: "",
                    Vrkme: ""
                });

                oModel.setProperty("/Items", aItems);
            },

            /* =========================================================== */
            /* REMOVE ITEM */
            /* =========================================================== */

            onRemoveItem: function () {

                var oTable = this.byId("itemTable");
                var oModel = this.getView().getModel("local");
                var aItems = oModel.getProperty("/Items");

                var aSelectedItems = oTable.getSelectedItems();

                if (aSelectedItems.length === 0) {
                    MessageToast.show("Select at least one item to delete");
                    return;
                }

                aSelectedItems.forEach(function (oItem) {
                    var sPath = oItem
                        .getBindingContext("local")
                        .getPath();

                    var iIndex = parseInt(sPath.split("/")[2]);
                    aItems.splice(iIndex, 1);
                });

                oModel.setProperty("/Items", aItems);
            },

            /* =========================================================== */
            /* SAVE (DEEP ENTITY CREATE) */
            /* =========================================================== */

            onSave: function () {
                debugger;

                var oModel = this.getOwnerComponent().getModel();
                var aItems = this.getView()
                    .getModel("local")
                    .getProperty("/Items");

                if (aItems.length === 0) {
                    MessageToast.show("Please add at least one item");
                    return;
                }

                var oPayload = {
                    Auart: this.byId("Aurat").getValue(),
                    Kunnr: this.byId("Kunnr").getValue(),
                    Erdat: new Date().toISOString().split("T")[0],
                    znavigation: aItems
                };

                oModel.create("/vbakSet", oPayload, {

                    success: (oData) => {

                        this._readLatestOrder();
                        MessageToast.show("Sales Order Created");

                        // Clear header fields
                        this.byId("Aurat").setValue("");
                        this.byId("Kunnr").setValue("");

                        // Clear items
                        this.getView()
                            .getModel("local")
                            .setProperty("/Items", []);
                    },

                    error: (oError) => {
                        MessageToast.show("Error while creating Sales Order");
                    }
                });
            },

            /* =========================================================== */
            /* READ LATEST ORDER */
            /* =========================================================== */

            _readLatestOrder: function () {

                var oModel = this.getOwnerComponent().getModel();

                oModel.read("/vbakSet", {
                    urlParameters: {
                        "$orderby": "Vbeln desc"
                    },

                    success: function (oData) {

                        if (oData.results.length > 0) {
                            var sVbeln = oData.results[0].Vbeln;

                            this.getOwnerComponent()
                                .getRouter()
                                .navTo("Detail", {
                                    Vbeln: sVbeln
                                });
                        }

                    }.bind(this),

                    error: function () {
                        MessageToast.show("Read failed");
                    }
                });
            }

        });
    }
);

 

  • Here view collects full data (header + items)  
  • The controller sends full data in one call (deep insert)  
  • This completes the deep entity flow for create scenario 

Output:

  •  Conclusion

Deep Entity simplifies handling header and item data in a single request, reducing multiple OData calls and keeping both backend and UI logic clean.

In this blog, we covered the complete lifecycle:

  • Backend setup using SEGW
  • Data retrieval using $expand
  • Deep insert (header + items in one call)
  • UI5 integration.

entry.title: Re: SAP Solution Map by Company Size
entry.content:
To answer your question, how they differ  

  • GROW with SAP:
    • Targets companies new to ERP or upgrading from lightweight systems.
    • Emphasizes speed, simplicity, and standardized processes.
    • Ideal for organizations that want to adopt SAP best practices quickly without heavy customization.
  • RISE with SAP:
    • Aimed at enterprises with legacy landscapes and complex requirements.
    • Bundles infrastructure, migration tools, and business process transformation.
    • Provides flexibility to run on hyperscalers (Azure, AWS, GCP) or SAP’s own cloud.
  • If your organization is medium-sized and growing, GROW with SAP is the right fit — it’s cost-effective and accelerates ERP adoption.
  • If you’re a large enterprise with multiple geographies, legacy systems, and complex processes, RISE with SAP is the recommended path.

entry.title: Implementing Determination Using Association in SAP RAP
entry.content:
Introduction

In SAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP), determinations are a powerful mechanism that automatically derive or calculate field values based on changes to other fields within a business object. They play a central role in enforcing business logic, keeping data consistent, and reducing manual input errors.

While determinations are commonly implemented using direct field references within the same entity, a more advanced and often necessary pattern involves leveraging associations — navigational links between related business object nodes or external CDS entities. This approach becomes essential when the value you need to derive depends not on the current entity alone, but on data that lives in an associated entity.

This blog walks through the concept and practical implementation of determinations that use associations in SAP RAP.

I have implemented a scenario where the non-editable Total Amount field in the header entity is automatically determined based on the Price and Quantity fields maintained in the associated item entity.

Below is the Header entity, which includes the Total Amount field

 

Below is the item entity, which includes quantity and price  field

 

 

In the behavior definition, the Total Amount field is configured as non-editable using feature control, ensuring that it cannot be explicitly modified by the user.

 

Determination has been defined in the behavior definition of the Item entity.

The determination logic is implemented in the local handler class of the Item entity.

 

  METHOD calculate_total_price.

    DATA lt_header_update TYPE TABLE FOR UPDATE zi_nk_header.

*Step 1 : Read changed items
    READ ENTITIES OF zi_nk_header IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY zi_nk_item
        FIELDS ( soid quantity price currency )
        WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
      RESULT DATA(lt_items)
      FAILED DATA(lf).

    DATA lt_header_keys TYPE TABLE FOR UPDATE zi_nk_header.

    LOOP AT lt_items ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<item>).
*Step 2 :Navigate to parent headers using back-association
      READ ENTITIES OF zi_nk_header IN LOCAL MODE
        ENTITY zi_nk_item BY \_header
          FIELDS ( soid totalamount currency )
          WITH VALUE #( ( %tky = <item>-%tky ) )
        RESULT DATA(lt_header_result)
        FAILED DATA(lf_h).

      LOOP AT lt_header_result ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<hdr>).
*Step 3 : Read all items under those headers
        READ ENTITIES OF zi_nk_header IN LOCAL MODE
          ENTITY zi_nk_header BY \_salesitem
            FIELDS ( quantity price currency )
            WITH VALUE #( ( %tky = <hdr>-%tky ) )
          RESULT DATA(lt_all_items)
          FAILED DATA(lf2).

*Step 4 : Calculate the total amount
*Here i have used decfloat34 to avoid the loss of decimal values and incorrect totals

        DATA(lv_total) = REDUCE decfloat34(
          INIT sum = CONV decfloat34( '0' )
          FOR <it> IN lt_all_items
          NEXT sum = sum + ( <it>-quantity * <it>-price )
        ).

        APPEND VALUE #(
          %tky        = <hdr>-%tky
          totalamount = lv_total
          currency    = <item>-currency
          %control    = VALUE #( totalamount = if_abap_behv=>mk-on )
        ) TO lt_header_update.

      ENDLOOP.
    ENDLOOP.

*Step 5 : Update the header entity
    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zi_nk_header IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY zi_nk_header
        UPDATE FIELDS ( totalamount currency )
        WITH lt_header_update
      FAILED DATA(lf3)
      REPORTED DATA(lr).

  ENDMETHOD.

 

The Total Amount and Currency fields are rendered as read-only, preventing any direct user modification.

In the Item entity, users provide input for Quantity and Price, based on which the Total Amount is determined and automatically updated in the associated Header entity.

 

Output:-

 

Conclusion:-

This approach ensures data consistency, reduces redundancy, and keeps the business logic modular and maintainable. It is especially useful in scenarios like calculating header totals based on item data, where changes in one entity automatically reflect in another.

entry.title: SAP GRC Process Control Job Scheduling Issues – SAP Notes & Keywords Guide
entry.content:
Automated Control Monitoring (ACM) jobs in SAP GRC Process Control often fail due to hidden technical, authorization, or configuration issues.

Instead of generic troubleshooting, this blog provides a keyword-driven SAP Notes mapping, allowing you to:

  • Search error text directly
  • Identify exact SAP Note
  • Fix issues faster

2. SAP Notes + Exact Keywords + Symptoms Mapping

🔴 A. Job Not Triggering / Scheduling Issues

SAP Note Encrypted (Search Terms) Symptoms Reference
3593052failed to create issue, reschedule job step failed, mc_failed_create_issue, issue creation error, job step retry failure, scheduler reschedule errorJob remains in Scheduled / not executed
3328050event-based monitoring issue not created, event scenario failure, no issue generated, monitoring job success but no issue, trigger failureJob not picked by scheduler
3312097integration evidence missing, MCP CCM integration issue, case integration incomplete, missing attachment/evidence, job step incompleteJob skipped / incomplete
3248870SQL deadlock, DB lock situation, data rollback, update termination, job dump during execution, LUW rollbackSilent failure / no execution
2919879wrong start time, mismatch with SM37, scheduling error, incorrect job start timestampJob missing / incorrect scheduling
2911406filter not applied, incorrect results, BR filter issueJob condition / logic issue

🔴 B. Job Execution Failures / Dumps

SAP Note Exact Keywords Symptoms
3426819CCM job error, module mc_failed_job_grp_ci_al_done, case integration failure, job log module error, CI integration issueDump during execution
3701175field offset too large dump, runtime error, internal table overflow, memory structure issue, job dumpRuntime dump
3695307runtime error during notification, inconsistent issue data, job failure during notification, monitoring inconsistencyJob stops midway
3694020timeframe truncated, incorrect period, job monitor shows wrong dates, incomplete timeframe displayUnexpected termination
3693482FAIL link short dump, UI action dump, navigation error, runtime dump on clickUI/runtime dump
3565749downtime scheduling issue, job executes immediately, incorrect scheduling after downtime, reschedule anomalyExecution anomaly
3525337BRF+ update not reflected, rule changes ignored, outdated rule execution, inconsistency in rule processingLogic failure
3446826notification missing, no email alert, event trigger failureNotification failure
2968656dependent job not triggered, chain job failure, workflow breakJob cancelled
3381181short dump MESSAGE_TYPE_X_TEXT, CL_GRPC_TESTLOG_API dump, case does not exist, ASSERT failure, runtime termination, job crashDump in execution

🔴 C. Data Fetch / Mapping Issues

SAP Note Exact Keywords Symptoms
3615018error in basic case API, job log error, CCM scheduling failure, configurable scenario dump, case API failure, job execution stopsMissing fields
3612749SOD failure, risk analysis failed, SOD job not executing, dependency failureIncorrect data
3572507duplicate email notifications, multiple alerts for same job, repeated failure emailsData inconsistency
3555614SOD results missing, blank job monitor, no data shown, evaluation missingBlank fields
3553362backend dump, job step runtime error, short dump during execution, technical dumpPartial data
3521866slow performance, job monitor lag, long execution time, UI delay, backend performance issueMissing attributes / slow fetch
3495734job logs missing, no logs in SLG1, queue logs not available, debugging issueData/log mismatch

🔴 D. Incorrect Results / Report Issues

SAP Note Exact Keywords Symptoms
2582160issue creation failure, no issue created, job error during issue creation, backend issue creation dumpOutput mismatch
2401507reschedule failed job steps not working, retry job step fails, scheduler limitation, job step stuckInconsistent results
2573820type BR does not exist, object not found, job execution failure due to missing BRMapping issue
2388122job step failed but deficiencies created, inconsistent result, partial failureMissing entries
2643942cancel job error, termination failure, scheduler cancel issueDisplay issue

🔴 E. Authorization Issues

SAP Note Exact Keywords Symptoms
3370349error during obsoleting job step, cannot obsolete job step, job step status failure, obsolete action dumpAuthorization-related failure
2064944UTC vs system time mismatch, wrong timezone display, job monitor time incorrect, SM37 mismatchExecution inconsistency
2010831navigation dump, job step link crash, UI dumpAccess/UI issue

🔴 F. Master Data / Configuration Issues

SAP Note Exact Keywords Symptoms
2846218job status not updated, status mismatch frontend/backend, job shows completed but actually error, inconsistent statusControls not picked
1909373wrong execution time, job monitor time mismatch, incorrect duration, scheduler vs monitor mismatchOrg/config issue
2129930job stuck in error status, automated job failure, job not completing, persistent error stateMissing assignments
1954054cancel job failure, event job cannot be stopped, scheduler issueConfig issue
2199574reschedule issue, job step retry not working, scheduler failureInvalid config

🔴 G. Performance / Miscellaneous

SAP Note Exact Keywords Symptoms
3630375internal server error, overwrite protected field, data source application error, HTTP 500 error, backend overwrite dumpPerformance / backend issue

3. How to Use This Effectively

  • Copy error text from:
    • Job log (SM37)
    • Dump (ST22)
    • Application log (SLG1)
  • Search using exact keywords above
  • Identify matching SAP Note
  • Implement fix via SNOTE

4. Real Case Mapping (Your Scenario)

Issue:
Risk category fields not coming

Keywords:

  • error in basic case API
  • case API failure
  • job execution stops

SAP Note: 3615018

5. Conclusion

Using SAP Notes + exact keywords + symptoms mapping provides a fast, reliable way to troubleshoot ACM job scheduling and execution issues in SAP GRC Process Control.

Hint:
#SAPGRC #ProcessControl #ACM #SAPNotes #ErrorHandling
@SAPSupport @GRCExperts @ABAPDevelopers

entry.title: Re: CM01 not pulling in unplanned orders
entry.content: OPA2 controls which categories of capacity requirements (planned orders, production orders) are considered in CM01, while OPU3 defines the scheduling parameters and which statuses of those orders (created, released, etc.) are relevant for capacity planning. So if you only see released production orders in CM01, it is because the OPU3 settings tied to your profile restrict capacity requirements to released status. To see all demand, your SAP team needs to adjust OPU3 so that “created” or “unreleased” production orders are also included, and then ensure your OPA2 profile references both planned and production orders.

entry.title: Re: Cloud ALM User
entry.content:
This perspective is absolutely accurate and gets to the heart of a sustainable IT strategy. The key question of where the actual work and knowledge building should take place is crucial for long-term success.

From a pragmatic point of view, Option 3 is the most future-proof approach, as it focuses on enablement and building internal capacity.

The limitations of projects: Project structures (as in Option 2) are inherently temporary. After go-live or the completion of an implementation, the project team disbands. Without targeted, early knowledge transfer, a critical support vacuum arises at precisely this point.

The necessity of internal operations: Especially with mission-critical and highly complex architectures (such as S/4HANA landscapes, extensive NetWeaver stacks, or Pacemaker clusters), the parent organization is absolutely dependent on this knowledge. Smooth operation, troubleshooting, and patch management require a deep understanding of the systems, which should not be covered permanently or exclusively by external resources.

The strategic value: By empowering its own organization, the company becomes independent. A short-term project result is thus transformed into a long-term, resilient operational capability.

entry.title: Re: Cost Difference Not Posted Automatically at Subcontra...
entry.content: It is not possible in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to automatically post the cost difference at subcontracting GR. The standard approach is to use Material Ledger / Actual Costing at period-end. Without ML, the behavior you observed (inventory at standard cost, no variance posting) is exactly how the system is designed.

entry.title: Preserving Critical Supplier Information: Transferring of Supplier Material Number (IDNLF)
entry.content:
Preserving Critical Supplier Information:

Why Transferring the Supplier Material Number (IDNLF) from Purchase Requisition to Purchase Order Matters

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, procurement processes are designed to be efficient, guided, and seamlessly integrated. From purchase requisition creation to purchase order execution, data consistency is key to ensuring smooth operational flows.

However, in everyday procurement scenarios, even small gaps in data transfer can lead to unnecessary effort, loss of information, and reduced user confidence. One such gap exists today in the handling of the Supplier Material Number (IDNLF) during the transition from Purchase Requisition (PR) to Purchase Order (PO).

________________________________________________________________________________________________________

The Business Scenario: A Common, Real‑World Process

Let’s look at a typical procurement flow:

  1. A Purchase Requisition is created in Manage Purchase Requisitions – Professional

    • A Desired Supplier is maintained
    • A Supplier Material Number (IDNLF) is entered at item level

  2. The requisition is processed in Process Purchase Requisitions

    • A Source of Supply is assigned (matching the Desired Supplier)

  3. A Purchase Order is created using Create Purchase Order

At this point, users expect all relevant and already‑maintained information to be transferred consistently.
However, the resulting Purchase Order does not contain the Supplier Material Number (IDNLF).

 

________________________________________________________________________________________________________

The Gap Today: Information Is Lost Along the Process

The Supplier Material Number is not a cosmetic field—it is often a business‑critical identifier used by suppliers for:

  • Order confirmation
  • Logistics and delivery processing
  • Invoice matching and clarification
  • Communication with supplier purchasing departments

When this field is entered manually in the Purchase Requisition but not transferred to the Purchase Order, important information is lost along an otherwise integrated process.

As a result:

  • Users need to re‑enter data manually
  • The risk of inconsistencies or errors increases
  • Additional clarification with suppliers becomes necessary

This breaks the promise of an end‑to‑end digital procurement process.

________________________________________________________________________________________________________

Why This Matters for Procurement Efficiency

In productive environments, Purchase Requisition processors handle high volumes of requisitions under time pressure. Every additional step reduces efficiency and increases frustration.

The missing transfer of IDNLF leads to:

  • Extra manual steps during PO creation or follow‑up changes
  • Reduced process speed
  • Potential quality issues in supplier communication

Instead of focusing on value‑adding tasks, users are forced to compensate for system limitations.

________________________________________________________________________________________________________

The Opportunity: Small Enhancement, High Value

The proposed improvement is straightforward:

Automatically transfer the Supplier Material Number (IDNLF) from the Purchase Requisition to the Purchase Order when a matching Source of Supply is assigned.

This ensures that:

  • All relevant procurement data is preserved throughout the process
  • The Purchase Order is complete from the moment it is created
  • Manual rework is eliminated

Technically simple, but with a disproportionately positive impact on daily procurement operations.

________________________________________________________________________________________________________

Expected Benefits

By closing this gap, SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud customers benefit from:

  • Improved efficiency and productivity
    Purchase Requisition processors can order faster with fewer steps in between.

  • Enhanced user experience
    Users trust that entered data is reliably carried forward.

  • Higher data quality
    Ensuring that no critical information is lost along the process.

  • Better supplier communication
    Suppliers receive Purchase Orders containing their own material references.

________________________________________________________________________________________________________

Call to Action: Support the Improvement Request

This improvement request is based on real customer usage. If you encounter similar challenges or agree that procurement data should remain consistent from requisition to order, I encourage you to:

👉 Please Vote to Idea # 343655 on SAP Influence and help move this enhancement forward.

entry.title: Re: No Mass Upload Option for Standard Text App – Possibl...
entry.content:
Good day @MarkusDaum 

Thank you for your question.

Below are answers to them.

Question 1: Is there any standard approach for mass uploading standard texts?

Answer: No, the Public Cloud does not provide this functionality. That app is centered on interactive maintenance in the Fiori app rather than a bulk import process.

Question 2: Are there recommended tools, reports, or APIs to handle this?

Answer: Migration Cockpit is the only standard tool for your business data migration, and it works with predefined migration objects.

Question 3: Has anyone implemented a custom solution (e.g. via LSMW, BAPI, or ABAP program)?

Answer: Considering its Public Cloud, the classic ECC or on-premise approach is not the right pattern. As you know LSMW is restricted and not recommended for S4H migrations, and for Public Cloud, it supported migration path as mentioned in the previous answer is the Migration Cockpit with predefined objects. In addition to that, the developer extensibility model SAP provides in Public Cloud is limited to a 3-system landscape and to released development objects and APIs. So, a classic unrestricted ABAP upload report or LSMW style loader is out of the recommended standard.

What that practically means for you is:

  • Manual maintenance of standard texts must be done in the app.
  • You can use standard texts in supported business scenarios, e.g., output related processes. See KBA 3524757 for more details.
  • You can carry out custom development that sits within the Public Cloud supported solutions.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Order Open on Month-End Not Considered for WIP – Partial Delivery & TECO in Next Period
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am facing an issue with WIP calculation for a production/process order in SAP.

Scenario:

  • Order status: Open as of 31st March
  • Partial delivery posted on 1st April
  • Variance calculation executed on 1st April
  • Order TECO’d on 2nd April

Despite the order being open at month-end (31st March), it was not considered in March WIP when tried doing CO88H that particular order was not considered. When checked with log in red you see is the order which was not considered. Settlement was run on 1st april so even if partial delivery was done. Rest of quantity WIP should have got calculated.

entry.title: Re: user created production order.Under production order...

entry.title: Re: Product Substitution :Controlling Substitution Priori...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question.

Here's what our internal chatbot replied. Can you please check whether it helps you resolve the issue?

Product Substitution provides a general overview of the existing functionality.

To achieve the required substitution logic where Product A is substituted by Products B, C, and D in a specific sequence, you can configure the substitution strategy using the following approach:

  1. Define Substitution Strategy: Supply Chain - Configuring the Optimal Substitution Method

    • Use the ‘Configure Substitution Strategy’ app to define a substitution strategy that specifies the sequence of substitutions. You can set Product B as the first substitute, followed by Product C, and finally Product D.

  2. Sequence Configuration: Supply Chain - Combining Plant and Product Substitution

    • Ensure that the sequence values are set correctly in the substitution master data. This will ensure that the system checks Product B first, then Product C, and finally Product D.

  3. Handling Stock Availability: Supply Chain - Product Substitution Data Retrieval Logic

    • For Product D, you can configure the system to always confirm the quantity regardless of stock availability. This can be done by setting a rule in the substitution strategy that overrides the availability check for Product D.

  4. Batch Determination:

    • After material confirmation, the system can determine the batch based on FIFO rules, which is standard behavior.

  5. Alternative Approach:

    • If the standard functionality does not fully meet your requirements, consider using custom logic or enhancements to achieve the desired behavior. The following API is available: Product Substitution.

Kind regards, Henrike

 

 

 

 

#AI-Generated Content

entry.title: Re: Vendor Details for RE document
entry.content:
FAGLL03H and FBL3N are GL-focused reports. They show postings at the G/L account level, but they don’t always carry the vendor/customer master data fields unless the posting itself was made directly to a vendor account. For RE documents, the system posts to reconciliation accounts or adjustment accounts, not directly to the vendor master. That’s why vendor details don’t appear in FAGLL03H or FBL3N. If you need to analyze vendor information for those postings, you should use vendor line item reports (like FBL1N) or drill down from the GL line item to the original FI document, where the vendor master data is stored.

If your business needs vendor details to show up in FAGLL03H/FBL3N for RE documents, you’d need a custom enhancement (e.g., adding vendor fields via coding block extension or report modification).

entry.title: Re: If the tax rate changes in an already created PO. The...
entry.content:
When you create a PO with a non‑deductible tax code, the system calculates the non‑deductible tax amount (EKPO‑NAVNW) at that point in time. Even if you later change the tax rate in the PO, the NAVNW field is not automatically re‑updated for valuation purposes. At Goods Receipt (MIGO), inventory is posted using the original NAVNW value (based on the tax rate at PO creation), which explains why your GRN valuation is still reflecting the 9% tax amount instead of the revised 5%.

This is not a bug; it is the intended design. To ensure correct valuation, SAP recommends either:

  • Re‑create the PO after tax rate changes, so NAVNW is recalculated.
  • Use Material Ledger / Actual Costing to adjust variances at period end.

If re‑creation is not feasible, a custom enhancement (e.g., BAdI for GR posting) would be needed to force recalculation of NAVNW at GR.

  • 3604090 - How to check historic information like table records, disk or memory size for certain table

Regards,
Laszlo

entry.title: Partner Determination Not Triggered in PO (ME21N) – EKPA Empty Despite Correct Configuration
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I seek your guidance on an issue related to partner determination in Purchase Orders.

System Context:

  • SAP ECC (MM – Purchasing)
  • Standard transaction: ME21N / ME23N
  • Custom document type: ZSO / ZCO
  • Partner schema assigned: Z002

Configuration Details:

  • Partner schema Z002 is correctly assigned to the PO document types (verified via OMEC)
  • Schema contains mandatory partner roles:
    • VN (Vendor)
    • PI (Invoicing Party)
    • OA (Ordering Address)

Issue Description:
While creating a Purchase Order using a specific vendor (e.g., 100173):

  • The Partners tab remains completely blank
  • No partner roles are automatically populated
  • Upon checking table EKPA, no entries are created for the PO
  • However, the PO is still saved successfully without errors

Observations:

  1. Configuration is consistent:
    • Partner schema assignment is correct
    • Mandatory roles are defined in schema

  2. Vendor master (WYT3) contains partner entries such as:

    • LF (Vendor)
    • BA (Goods Supplier)
    • RS (Invoicing-related role)

    However, roles like PI and OA are not explicitly maintained.

  3. No custom developments, BAPI, or interface is involved in PO creation (manual ME21N)

Expected Behavior:
System should determine and populate partner roles (VN, PI, OA) in the PO based on vendor master and schema.

Actual Behavior:

  • Partner determination does not trigger
  • EKPA remains empty
  • Partners tab shows no data

Clarification Required:

  1. Does partner determination strictly require exact partner function codes (e.g., PI, OA) in vendor master, or can equivalent roles (e.g., RS, BA) be considered?
  2. In cases where mandatory roles are missing in vendor master, should the system:
    • Prevent PO creation?
    • Or allow PO creation but skip partner determination?
  3. Is there any standard SAP behavior where partner determination silently fails without error if required roles are not found?

Any insights or similar experiences would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Ayaz Khan

entry.title: Re: Inventory Packaging Materials Set-up guidance
entry.content:
Good day @Kevin12 

Thank you for your question.

Based on what you described, the experience is expected when you only use the packaging material to create the handling unit during packing, but no packaging item is generated in the outbound delivery.

Note that the system does not simply reduce the packaging stock simply because you created a HU in Pack Outbound Deliveries. For the packaging material to be consumed at PGI, the system must create a delivery item for the packaging material. You must also define the storage bin from which the packaging material is taken in Determine Goods Movement Bin.

KBA 2818830 explicitly refers to the creation of an Automatically Generated Packaging Item during goods issue in EWM. If you do not properly create the packaging item, GI related issues will occur.

Regarding your statement “e really need further instructions on how to configure the inventory to be consumed on Outbound Deliveries/PGI”, here are areas you should focus on:

  1. To ensure packaging inventory is consume on outbound delivery/PGI, you must configure packaging item generation. If you only pack with material PCKINVT in the work center and no packaging item is generated in the outbound delivery, then PGI will reduce the finished goods but not the packaging stock.
  2. At minimum you need to validate these points:
    1. Your packaging product type VERP is correct in principle, but VERP in Public Cloud depends on the relevant EWM scope activation. See KBA 2807296 for more details.
    2. You must generate delivery items for packaging materials. This requirement is the central requirement.
    3. As mentioned above, you must define the storage bin from which the packaging material is taken. See KBA 2818830 for more insights.
  3. Based on your description, the most likely reason for the packaging material not being reduced on the Post Goods Issue is this:
    • PCKINVT is available as a packaging material in the packing app
    • The system creates the HU
    • But the system does not create a consumable packaging delivery item
    • Therefore, PGI posts only the goods issue for the packed product, but not for PCKINVT.

Finally, as a next step, you should check the outbound delivery item list after packing, verify whether PCKINVT appears as a packaging item, and then review Determine Goods Movement Bin in your configuration. If no packaging item is generated, then that is the main reason your inventory is not consumed during PGI. This is what KBA 2818830 points to.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Partner Determination Not Triggered in PO (ME21N) – E...
entry.content:
How Partner determination works is the partner schema (Z002) defines which partner functions must be present in the PO (VN, PI, OA, etc.). At PO creation, the system looks into the vendor master partner functions (table WYT3) to pull the corresponding roles. If the vendor master does not contain the exact partner functions defined in the schema, the system cannot populate them in the PO.

In your case, Vendor master has LF, BA, RS. Schema requires VN, PI, OA. Since PI and OA are missing in the vendor master, the system cannot determine them. Hence, the result is EKPA stays empty, Partners tab is blank. SAP standard does not throw an error at PO creation; it allows saving even if mandatory schema roles are not found. The “mandatory” flag only enforces that the field must be filled if the partner function is present, not that the system blocks creation if it cannot be determined.

Maintain the missing partner functions (PI, OA) in the vendor master (Purchasing view → Partner functions). Or adjust the partner schema to align with the roles actually maintained in vendor master. If you want the system to block PO creation when mandatory partners are missing, you’d need a custom enhancement (e.g., user exit in ME21N/ME22N).

entry.title: Re: Updated Key field length of database and when TR is r...
entry.content:
Good day @ayechan 

Thank you for your question.

Based on your description and the error message, your CDS object ZC_ITEM was already treated as a released API for the Use in Key User Apps and Use in Cloud Development. So, once an object is released under C1, SAP protects its public interface from incompatible changes such as existing parameters, elements, associations and their technical data types.

In your case, the transport release fails because the compatibility check found that a key element was added. Which the system considers as mentioned above as an incompatible change for a released API.

Here are references for more guidance

https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_cp_index_htm/CLOUD/en-US/ABENABAP_RELEASE_CONTRACTS.html

https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_816_index_htm/8.16/en-US/ABENRESTRICTED_APIS_ATC_COMPA.html

So, this basically means:

  • Adding a normal non key field can be acceptable in some cases.
  • Adding a key field or changing a released key definition is not acceptable once the object is already released and checked against a snapshot.
  • Changing the length of a released key field is also risky for the same reason, because the technical type and interface stability are protected.

How to fix it depends on two scenarios, and the right one depends on whether this object was already truly delivered and should remain stable.

Option 1: A quick workaround, only if you have not yet delivered any API that needs to stay stable, SAP allows you to make the default API snapshot not check relevant in the Manage API Snapshots app. See below. KBA 3442779 provides more details on that.

Option 2, which is also best practice and long-term correction solution is incase the object is already meant to be a stable released API, then do not change the released key. Instead of that:

  1. Keep the old, released object unchanged.
  2. Create a new CDS/service version with the new key design.
  3. Release the new object and,
  4. Adapt your app or consumers to the new object.

If you only need a fast correction in a project sandbox and the API was not yet really delivered, use Manage API Snapshots and set the default snapshot to not check relevant, then create your own snapshot later once the API is stable. KBA 3442779 also mentions this pattern.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

"SAP GenAI was used to help generate this content"

entry.title: Re: Issue while creating Mass Pack Specification through...
entry.content:
Dear @Vedant_Chaudhari,

The standard SAP-supported approach in this area is Packing Instruction maintenance through the standard apps, plus the released Packing Instruction Read, Create (A2X) API. SAP documents Manage Packing Instructions Master Data for searching existing packing instructions and navigating to create/edit and it documents the Create Packing Instruction process in Public Cloud as well. For the specific issue with /o/SCWM/IPU and report /SCWM/R_PS_DATA_LOAD this means that in Public Cloud this upload should not be positioned as the standard tool for ongoing mass change or upsert of existing records

The key point is that the released API is documented as Read, Create (A2X) and its documented operations are creation-oriented. Accordingly the standard Public Cloud design supports:

  • creation of new packing instructions via API and
  • maintenance of existing packing instructions in the apps
    rather than a standard Excel-based mass upsert/update process with rollback or stop-on-error behavior for this object

As a result this process should not be positioned in Public Cloud as a standard mass maintenance mechanism with full rollback or stop-on-error behavior. Likewise, re-uploading corrected data should not be treated as a standard method to change an existing record so creation of a duplicate is consistent with a create-oriented process

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/60c80a4601c340b983cdba5c1a2f8aa8.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/6c5db9537cceb44ce10000000a174cb4.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/758cbf53f106b44ce10000000a174cb4.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/9ce855c709ce4f5c99b685903461106e/8c64ea338f6a4c68b40f8dbcafde8954.html

The recommended workaround is:

  • use Excel only as an external staging file
  • check first whether the packing instruction already exists
  • send new records through the released API
  • and maintain existing/corrected records in Manage / Change Packing Instruction instead of re-uploading them as new

Summary:

In Public Cloud, the supported model for this object is standard app maintenance for changes and Read/Create API for new records. Therefore the recommended design is API-based creation for new packing instructions and app-based correction of existing ones, rather than file-based mass upsert through /SCWM/R_PS_DATA_LOAD

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: I tricked Datasphere into running my custom scaler functions
entry.content:
@albertosimeoni ,

Regarding 3.1:

Yes, you are correct - in theory. 
The problem is, that the official procedure does not - for reasons I have not quite been able to figure out - it does not get the permissions quite right. 

Calling it with 'EXECUTE' instead of 'SELECT' should do the trick, but I coulnd't get it to work consistently. 

Regarding 3.2:

Oh how I wish you were right. 
The supported SQL functions is tied to the DB version, and there is no "SUPPORTED_FUNCTIONS" table / view of the built-in SQL functions. There are "FUNCTIONS" tables, but those are UDF functions, not the built-in functions. 

Datasphere itself is, I'm willing to bet, a CAP app, built on SAPUI5 and TypeScript.
When Typescript is deployed, it is transpiled to JavaScript, which is why we get the "chunk" files. 
Transpiled TypeScript is transformed and optimized in a number of ways that makes it a bit hard to "guess" what the original code looked like, without access to the source files. Which I doubt SAP is willing to send me. 

However, in the transpiled JavaScript, the functions is just a hardcoded list. It could be a DB table in the original code, which is then abstracted away in deployment, but there is no way to determine for sure, or find that table. 

So, the steps in 3.2 are hacky as all hell - but it's the only way.
